explanation: Dimensions are \([P]=ML^{-1}T^{-2}\), \([G]=M^{-1}L^3T^{-2}\), \([c]=LT^{-1}\), and \([\hbar]=ML^2T^{-1}\). Let \(P=G^a c^b \hbar^d\). Comparing powers of M, L, T gives \(-a+d=1\), \(3a+b+2d=-1\), and \(-2a-b-d=-2\). Solving, \(a=-2\), \(b=7\), and \(d=-1\). Hence \(P\propto G^{-2}c^7\hbar^{-1}=\dfrac{c^7}{\hbar G^2}\).

Use \([S]=MT^{-2}\), \([\rho]=ML^{-3}\), \([g]=LT^{-2}\), and \([\eta]=ML^{-1}T^{-1}\). For \(l=S^a\rho^b g^c\eta^d\), comparing powers with \([l]=L\) gives \(a+b+d=0\), \(-3b+c-d=1\), and \(-2a-2c-d=0\). Solving in terms of d gives \(a=(2-3d)/4\). For a to be an integer, d must satisfy \(d\equiv 2\pmod 4\). The smallest positive value is \(d=2\). Then \(a=-1\), \(b=-1\), and \(c=0\). Thus \(l\propto S^{-1}\rho^{-1}\eta^2=\dfrac{\eta^2}{S\rho}\).
